name: Kamananui (Moanalua) Valley to Dam
tagline: A flat, shady valley walk perfect for families who want nature without the workout.
parking: Free parking at the Moanalua Valley Park trailhead off Moanalua Valley Road
bestTime: Any time of day — the valley stays cool and shaded
talkStory: Moanalua Valley was the beloved country estate of Prince Lot Kapuaiwa (King Kamehameha V), who hosted elaborate garden parties here in the 1860s. The massive monkeypod trees along the trail are remnants of his royal grounds, and the famous Hitachi Tree (featured in Japanese commercials for decades) stands near the park entrance. Ancient Hawaiians cultivated taro in this valley — you can spot remnants of old lo'i walls along the stream. The easy 3-mile walk to the dam is the gentle beginning of the epic trail to the Ko'olau summit.
